concepts.title: Training-serving skew vs feature drift
concepts.blurb: Skew is training vs production. Drift is production vs production-yesterday. Memorize this verbatim — it's the §6.2 anchor.

concepts.title: Feature Store sunsets (Feb 17, 2027)
concepts.blurb: Legacy + Optimized online serving sunset Feb 17, 2027. Bigtable online serving = the safe exam answer.

concepts.title: Vizier hyperparameter tuning
concepts.blurb: Vertex AI Vizier wraps custom training with a StudySpec. Algorithms: Bayesian (default), Random, Grid. parallelTrialCount × maxTrialCount; high parallelism + Bayesian = degraded learning.

concepts.title: Vertex AI Experiments + ML Metadata
concepts.blurb: Experiments tracks runs/metrics/params for comparison. ML Metadata tracks artifacts + lineage. Question pattern: 'compare 5 training runs' → Experiments.

concepts.title: Responsible AI + security (Explainable AI deprecation)
concepts.blurb: Vertex Explainable AI deprecated Mar 16, 2026; shutdown Mar 16, 2027 — still tested in v3.1. Sampled Shapley (tabular default), Integrated Gradients (NN), XRAI (image).

concepts.title: Serving deep-dive (online vs batch vs Dataflow vs BQML)
concepts.blurb: Online = Vertex Endpoint (low latency). Batch = no endpoint, BQ output. Dataflow inference = streaming. BQML = SQL inside BigQuery.

concepts.title: IAM + VPC-SC + private endpoints
concepts.blurb: Service accounts for training vs prediction. Workload Identity for GKE-side training. VPC-SC perimeters around Vertex resources. PSC for private endpoints.

concepts.title: AutoML endpoint scale-to-zero trap
concepts.blurb: AutoML endpoints CANNOT scale to zero — idle classification endpoint ≈ $991/month. Common §4 distractor: 'deploy AutoML to Vertex endpoint' when batch prediction would do.

concepts.title: Gemini SFT = LoRA-based PEFT
concepts.blurb: Gemini supervised fine-tuning is LoRA under the hood. Adapter sizes 1/2/4/8/16. Only 2.5 Pro/Flash/Flash-Lite tunable. Pro maxes at adapter 8. Preference tuning (RLHF-style) is Flash and Flash-Lite only.

products.title: Vertex AI Reduction Server
products.blurb: Vertex-only NCCL all-reduce algorithm. +75% throughput uplift on multi-GPU training, no code changes. The single highest-yield distinguishing topic on the exam.

products.title: Vertex AI Pipelines
products.blurb: Serverless KFP runner. $0.03/run + compute, no idle cost. Default §5 orchestrator pick.

products.title: Cloud Composer 3
products.blurb: Managed Airflow. $0.06/DCU-hr × ~$400/mo floor on smallest env. Pick when you need cron, multi-system orchestration, or heavy non-ML scheduling.

products.title: Vector Search (formerly Matching Engine)
products.blurb: ScaNN-based ANN store on Vertex AI. Renamed Aug 2023. Key §1.2 GenAI/RAG component for embedding retrieval.

products.title: Vertex AI Model Monitoring v2
products.blurb: Announced Jun 11 2024, still Pre-GA Apr 2026. Attaches to Model Registry version (not endpoint), supports off-Vertex models, on-demand jobs, attribution drift first-class. v1 default drift threshold = 0.3; v2 uses smaller defaults.

products.title: Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform (formerly Vertex AI Agent Builder)
products.blurb: End-to-end RAG + tool-use agents. Renamed Apr 22 2026 — exam may use either name. Vertex AI Agent Engine is the managed agent runtime.

products.title: TPU machine types on Vertex AI
products.blurb: ct5lp-hightpu-{1t,4t,8t} (v5e), ct6e-standard (v6e). v5e/v5p/v6e are safe exam answers. B200/H200/GB200/A4/Ironwood may not be in v3.1 scope yet.

decision_trees.title: Which compute? CPU / GPU / TPU / Reduction Server
decision_trees.blurb: CPU = small models, debugging, batch. GPU = >50M params, dense matmul. TPU = TF/JAX, dense, large batch. Reduction Server = Vertex GPUs only, +75% throughput.

decision_trees.title: Which orchestrator? Vertex Pipelines / Composer / Kubeflow / MLFlow / TFX
decision_trees.blurb: Cost-driven pick: Vertex Pipelines (cheapest), Composer (cron + heavy non-ML), Kubeflow on GKE (custom), TFX (TF-only). Highest §5 question driver.

decision_trees.title: Which tabular approach? BQML / AutoML / custom training
decision_trees.blurb: BQML = SQL-resident, fastest. AutoML = no code, traps include scale-to-zero ($991/mo idle). Custom training = full control, most expensive.
